Name: Asha Weinstein Agrawal (linkedin)
College: School of Planning, Policy, and Environmental Studies.
Title: Professor
Research_Interests: Research Interests: Her research agenda is guided by a commitment to the principles of sustainability and equity: what planning and policy tools can communities adopt to encourage environmentally-friendly travel and improve accessibility for people struggling with poverty or other disadvantages? she has explored this question most deeply through two substantive areas, Transportation finance policy and the travel behavior of pedestrians, Cyclists, And transit riders
Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: Shes mostly teaches a masters-level capstone class.
Sustainability_Contributions: Research on what planning and policy tools can communities adopt to encourage environmentally-friendly travel. She has been invited to share her research findings at events such as a briefing session for staff of the U.S. Senate Committee on Environment and Public Works.
Research_Interests_List: Research Interests: Her research agenda is guided by a commitment to the principles of sustainability and equity: what planning and policy tools can communities adopt to encourage environmentally-friendly travel and improve accessibility for people struggling with poverty or other disadvantages? she has explored this question most deeply through two substantive areas, Transportation finance policy and the travel behavior of pedestrians, Cyclists, And transit riders

Name: Athanasios "Alex" Alexandrou
Title: Professor
Research_Interests: Research Interests: Electrification of agricultural equipment, Air quality for agricultural operations, Truck emission control systems, Cold plasma technology and its applications in agriculture, And automation.
Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: Industrial control systems, Automation.
Sustainability_Contributions: Air quality mitigation and the monitoring of pollutants within agricultural production practices. Work in air quality involves extensive research into the monitoring and suppression of fugitive dust, particulate matter (PM2.5 and PM10), and organic compounds such as fungal spores and pollen during agricultural field operations. Current work involves the Heavy-Duty Inspection and Maintenance Referee Program (Clean Truck Check) and analyzing agricultural equipment activity data to better understand and manage sector-wide emissions. Served as a consultant on agricultural electrification needs in California and spearheaded research into the environmental benefits of biomass production for the European Union in 2011.
Research_Interests_List: Research Interests: Electrification of agricultural equipment, Air quality for agricultural operations, Truck emission control systems, Cold plasma technology and its applications in agriculture, And automation.

Name: Andy Chang
Title: Adjunt Professor and Program Leader at Smithsonian Environmental Research Center West
Research_Interests: Research Interests: How geographic patterns of diversity in estuarine and coastal systems are controlled by environmental drivers and biotic interactions. use of estuaries as a laboratory to understand human influences on coastal ecosystems and to test fundamental ecological theories about the factors that control species diversity, Abundance, And ecosystem functioning. work in the marine invasions lab focuses on the influence of freshwater flow and temperature variation on marine invertebrate community assembly, Biogeography, Invasion patterns, And native oyster population dynamics.
Sustainability_Contributions: Biodiversity, ecology, estuarine ecology, global change, invasive species, marine ecology, marine invertebrates, oysters.

Name: Alison O'Dowd
Title: Professor

Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: Environmental science and ecological restoration.

Expert research on stream and wetland restoration, including studies of benthic macroinvertebrates as indicators of water quality—used for developing bioassessment indices. Pioneering work on dam removal impacts, including a major project funded ($814 k) by the California Wildlife Conservation Board to examine effects of the largest dam removal in U.S., focusing on salmonid food webs, water quality, and informing future dam‑removal ecology. Plays a significant role in student sustainability training, mentoring undergraduates and graduate students in applied field research.

Sustainability_Contributions: Contributed to Fifth California Climate Change Assessment for North Coast. Water-quality monitoring & sediment budget research, using instruments, erosion plots, and radionuclide tracers to measure the fate and movement of fine sediment and nutrients. Climate‑change and thermal-stream studies: modeling stream temperature responses to riparian reforestation and climate warming using distributed sensing and HEAT SOURCE models to support salmonid habitat conservation. Securing and deploying research funding for field-intensive, restoration-oriented studies that inform sustainable forest and watershed management across northern California. Coordinating lead author of the north coast regional report, part of the 5th climate assessment.

Name: Anne Marie Todd
Title: Dean and Professor
Research_Interests: Research Interests: Sense of place, Environmental activism, Climate pedagogy
Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: Environmental communication, Rhetoric, And popular culture
Sustainability_Contributions: Dean Todd Developed an interdisciplinary course on Global Climate Change. Her work examines the way environmental rhetoric shapes our community history and sense of place. Her most recent book, Valley of Heart's Delight: Environment and Sense of Place in the Santa Clara Valley, focuses on the agricultural history of Silicon Valley. Her book, Communicating Environmental Patriotism: A Rhetorical History of the American Environmental Movement received the Christine Oravec Environmental Communication Research Award. She has also published more than 30 scholarly journal articles and book chapters, including research on climate literacy, gardening and food activism, and environmental messages in popular culture. Dean Todd is a founding member of the International Environmental Communication Association and served on the advisory board of the Green Ninja Project. Former chair of the University Sustainability Board.
Research_Interests_List: Research Interests: Sense of place, Environmental activism, Climate pedagogy
